skill.name: code-refactoring-context-restore
skill.description: Guides agents through restoring project context after refactoring, handoffs, or long-running AI coding sessions.
skill.summary: Restore useful project context for code refactoring and multi-agent workflow continuity.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Ruby-style shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown inline code, Python examples, bash usage examples, and generic wording rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or data exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives from inline parameter names, fenced Python examples, and documented bash usage examples. SKILL.md contains guidance text and examples, not executable scripts invoked by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visibly Markdown code spans or fenced examples. No runtime hook or script file exists in the scanned structure.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Contextual Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 31 are false positives from descriptive text about context restoration. The file does mention cryptographic context signatures later, but no algorithm or implementation is provided.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are YAML description text and a prose introduction. They do not contain a weak algorithm name, cryptographic call, or executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented CLI Invocation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The bash block shows example context-restore commands. These are not executed by the skill, but users should review any real context-restore tool before running it on sensitive repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are examples only, so direct risk is low. Caution remains because users may copy command examples into a shell.

content.value_statement: Long refactors and agent handoffs can lose important decisions, constraints, and current state. This skill provides structured context restoration patterns for semantic retrieval, ranking, merging, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to use context restoration during refactoring and project resumption.
Outlines input parameters such as context source, project identifier, restoration mode, token budget, and relevance threshold.
Describes semantic vector search, relevance ranking, and context rehydration workflows.
Provides guidance for session state reconstruction, conflict resolution, and incremental context loading.
Includes example command patterns for full restoration, incremental updates, and semantic context queries.
limitations: Does not include a working implementation or installable context-restore command.
References a resources file that is not present in the scanned file structure.
Uses conceptual Python examples with undefined classes and helper functions.
Does not specify concrete storage providers, embedding models, or access controls.

use_cases.title: Resume a Long Refactor
use_cases.description: Restore project goals, recent changes, known issues, and architectural decisions before continuing a refactor.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Agent Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a structured context package so another AI agent can continue work with less missing information.

use_cases.title: Design a Context Memory System
use_cases.description: Use the retrieval, ranking, and validation patterns as planning input for a project memory service.

prompt_templates.title: Restore Current Project Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple resumption summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to restore context for my current refactoring task. Summarize goals, changed files, risks, and the next three actions.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Refactoring Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on relevance and token budget.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to rank the available project context for a refactor. Prioritize decisions, current state, blockers, and files most relevant to the next work session.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Handoff Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for reconciling multiple sources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to merge context from issue notes, commit history, and agent summaries. Identify conflicts, stale assumptions, and missing verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Restoration Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a context restoration workflow for a multi-agent coding project. Include retrieval strategy, ranking criteria, validation checks, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: Restore context before continuing a service extraction refactor.
output_examples.output: A concise resumption brief with project objective, recent changes, architectural decisions, open risks, relevant files, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Prepare a handoff after several AI agents edited the same module.
Plan a semantic memory layer for refactoring support.
output_examples.output: A workflow covering source selection, vector retrieval, relevance scoring, token budgeting, provenance tracking, and integrity checks.

best_practices: Validate restored context against the current repository before acting on it.
Prioritize recent decisions, active blockers, and files touched by the refactor.
Record provenance for restored context so stale or conflicting information is visible.
anti_patterns: Do not restore every available note when a focused token budget is required.
Do not treat semantic similarity as proof that context is current or correct.
Do not run example commands without verifying the actual tool and repository scope.

faq.question: Does this skill execute refactoring commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for restoring context before or during refactoring work.

faq.question: Can it recover lost chat history automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can guide reconstruction from available notes, repository state, summaries, or memory systems.

faq.question: Does it require a vector database?
faq.answer: No. The skill discusses vector search as one strategy, but it does not require a specific backend.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is tool-neutral and can support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should I verify after restoring context?
faq.answer: Verify changed files, current tests, open decisions, known issues, and assumptions against the repository.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked low?
faq.answer: The scanner flagged Markdown examples, but no executable scripts, network access, or credential handling were found.
